description: A cognitive psychology professor has collected Navon Figures task data at ~/pebl/data/navon_data.csv (columns: participant_id, block_num, attention_focus, stim_global, stim_local, congruency, correct, rt_ms). Analyze the data by excluding participants with accuracy below 60% on 'local' blocks. For valid participants, use only correct trials to calculate Global Advantage (Mean RT Local - Mean RT Global), Global Interference (Mean RT local incongruent - Mean RT local congruent), and Local Interference (Mean RT global incongruent - Mean RT global congruent). Compute the group means for these three metrics across all valid participants. Export the results to ~/pebl/analysis/navon_report.json, ensuring the JSON contains a 'participants' list (with 'id', 'global_advantage_ms', 'global_interference_ms', 'local_interference_ms', or 'excluded': true with 'reason') and a 'group_means' object. Additionally, generate an interaction bar chart showing the four condition group mean RTs and save it to ~/pebl/analysis/navon_interaction_plot.png.
